title: Anthropic keeps new AI model private after it finds thousands of external vulnerabilities

raw_body:
Anthropic’s most capable AI model has already found thousands of AI cybersecurity vulnerabilities across every major operating system and web browser. The company’s response was not to release it, but to quietly hand it to the organisations responsible for keeping the internet running. That model is Claude Mythos Preview, and the initiative is called Project Glasswing. […]
The post Anthropic keeps new AI model private after it finds thousands of external vulnerabilities appeared first on AI News.
speedrun: Anthropic has decided not to release its latest AI model, Claude Mythos Preview, after it identified thousands of cybersecurity vulnerabilities across major operating systems and web browsers. Instead, the company has shared the findings with organizations responsible for internet security through an initiative called Project Glasswing. This approach highlights the growing importance of cybersecurity in AI development and how companies are prioritizing safety over immediate public access.
why_it_matters: This decision directly impacts cybersecurity teams who can now address vulnerabilities before they are exploited, enhancing internet safety.
On a broader scale, it reflects a shift in the AI industry towards prioritizing security, potentially influencing how future models are developed and released.

lenses.eli12: Anthropic found many security issues with its new AI model but chose not to share it publicly. Instead, they gave the information to internet safety organizations. This means they want to help keep everyone safer online, which is something that could benefit all internet users.
lenses.pm: For product managers and founders, this situation emphasizes the importance of security in AI development. By focusing on vulnerabilities before public release, companies can build trust and create safer products. This could lead to a more cautious approach in future AI launches, balancing innovation with responsibility.
lenses.engineer: From a technical perspective, Claude Mythos Preview has identified thousands of vulnerabilities across major platforms, showcasing its analytical capabilities. This model's findings are significant for cybersecurity, as they provide a proactive approach to addressing potential threats. However, the decision to keep it private raises questions about transparency and collaboration in the AI field.
